subject: Geography
title: Tokyo: The World's Largest
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Tokyo: The World's Largest.

hook.text: Greater Tokyo holds 37 million people—more than the entire population of Canada.

buildup.text: Its train network moves 40 million passengers per day with legendary punctuality.

discovery.text: Tokyo rebuilt itself after the 1923 earthquake and WWII firebombing—twice leveled, twice reborn.

twist.text: Japan's population is shrinking. Tokyo grows while rural towns disappear.

climax.text: Tokyo is testing the limits of how large a city can grow when the country around it shrinks.

punchline.text: The world's biggest city is in a country that's getting smaller.

quiz.question: How many people live in Greater Tokyo?
